item.title: Mod Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Blacksmoker_in_Atlantic_Ocean.jpg

Image Source, Public Domain

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A friend pointed this out to me: Apparently sometimes, life imitates your shitposty SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a "hit" or visitor counter that only started counting after the first Foundation recon. And it shows a number higher than the number of Foundation visits as of the last one, meaning we aren't the only ones paying attention to the messages.

Perfect to go along with the clickbait messages.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Let's admit. We all have a little bit of a guilty pleasure going onto Buzzfeeds you tube channel.

1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Am I supposed to know what the device with seven digit represent, or are the hints deliberately left out to lead us to a logical dead end?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: man i could go for some pelagi cola

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 10/10 would click on adorable pompeii worms

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't quite know where I stand on this. I was a little confused by the premise at first, and then I think I kept expecting there to be a little bit more explanation. Just a direction for the black box that would point the imagination to a culprit. If there was a reason this was happening, albeit a crazy and alien hypothesis, I would be a lot happier.

Radical corporate environmentalists trying to placate Gaia, f'rinstance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The only thing I can't say I like is crosslinking to three specific articles. I'm fine with crosslinks when they're done well, and this isn't done badly, but it just seems off.

That's actually one of the best things about this article. It only seems off because we've been telling each other for literally years that this actually good thing is terrible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Plausible procedures and accurate journal article titles really turn my crank. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm kinda sorry the comments explain the black box, because that really had my gears whirring by the end. Still a fun little doodad, and if nothing else, I'm impressed that you sold me on the method of detection.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure where I stand on this. It's well executed, but the appeal is just not there for me. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's… okay, but doesn't really do anything for me. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is more or less my opinion on the piece as well. There were many interesting directions that it could have gone (off the top of my head, "who is this being made for?" "why is it in Spanish?"). Instead we just have "what if clickbait had to do exclusively with ocean things?."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The fact that something like "BUY PALEGI-COLA" repeated twice is so funny to me, despite it being just as funny as the other "headlines", speaks volume to the humor here. Somehow, everything about this is funny despite it working seriously as a mainlister and the humor is consistent throughout the skip, which is itself quality.

The only thing I can't say I like is crosslinking to three specific articles. I'm fine with crosslinks when they're done well, and this isn't done badly, but it just seems off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can we all just agree that this is a rare SCP that has no story but is freaking great
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Delightfully weird and unusual. No apparant story, but this one probobly doesn't need it.

Just like the SCP that requires LARPing to contain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the fact that when this:

EVIDENCE THAT ALIENS VISITED EARTH THOUSANDS OF YEARS AGO


happened, the Foundation's reaction was, "No shit, Sherlock. Tell us something new."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for ADORABLE BABY POMPEII WORMS and also the fact that I get way, way too distracted on the internet…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's… buzzfeed in the ocean. It's weird, but it didn't really hold my interest, and the idea seemed to actually be pretty shallow once you get over the initial "huh, this is kind of weird" factor. It seems like you were sort of trying to go for something deeper going on here, but there aren't enough breadcrumbs to piece anything together about it. Did like the inclusion of scientific journals, but I ultimately can't upvote an SCP I don't like for including an element I wish more SCPs would use.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…Hydrothermal clickbait.

Take my +1, you magnificent bastard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love it. A black smoker vent that is giving off targeted 'buzz feed' style headlines… aimed at the OCEAN.

+1. Horrifying? No. Weird? YES. I deeply enjoyed the highly technical explanation juxtaposed with bizarre clickbait.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure how I feel about this, it's slightly interesting, but I'm not quite sure what's anomalous about it. I'm saving my vote for now
